title: In Silico Prediction of the Impact of Genomic Variations in the Small Conductance Calcium Activated Potassium Channel SK3 Structure and Function
description_es: Este estudio utiliza herramientas de IA como AlphaFold2 y ColabFold para modelar estructuras proteicas de variantes del gen KCNN3, analizando las consecuencias estructurales y funcionales de variaciones genéticas del canal SK3 relacionadas con enfermedades neurodegenerativas y psiquiátricas.
abstract: The small conductance calcium-activated potassium channel SK3 (KCNN3) plays a crucial role in neuronal excitability and has been implicated in various neuropsychiatric disorders. This study employs AI-based structure prediction tools (AlphaFold2, ColabFold) to model the structural and functional consequences of KCNN3 genetic variations. We identified four coding splice variants and 13 pathogenic missense mutations, providing insights into the molecular mechanisms underlying SK3-related neurological conditions.

citations.apa: Padilla, L., del Val, C., Neidre, D. B., Kokenge, A. S., Martinez, J. E., Teixeira, A. L., Zwir, I., & de Erausquin, G. A. (2025). In Silico Prediction of the Impact of Genomic Variations in the Small Conductance Calcium Activated Potassium Channel SK3 Structure and Function. Frontiers in Neuroscience, 19, 1631536.
citations.mla: Padilla, L., et al. "In Silico Prediction of the Impact of Genomic Variations in the Small Conductance Calcium Activated Potassium Channel SK3 Structure and Function." Frontiers in Neuroscience, vol. 19, 2025, p. 1631536.
